element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Prince Myer begins comparatively weak, throwing only one sword at a time
Earned by killing monsters; establishes shop economy loop

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Early dungeon maze (hidden dungeons before palace)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Fixed locations with changing inventories; players learn to plan purchases

element_introduction_order.first_appears: 10 hidden dungeons en route to main palace
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Requires trial-and-error exploration; teaches nonlinear navigation
Seven towers total; each contains boss and collectable bell
One boss per tower; defeat required to progress toward palace center
Bells must be burned here to destroy towers and progress
Alternate versions of towers; contain superior equipment
Superior equipment availability; optional high-risk content
Permanent progression mechanic; blurs action-adventure and RPG mechanics
Unlocked only upon completing seven-tower sequence; ultimate challenge

typical_relief_triggers: Finding secret rooms with superior gear
typical_tension_triggers: Hidden dungeon maze traversal (167-235 screens per dungeon)
Enemy encounters requiring multiple hits (8+ hits for some enemies early game)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Long exploration cycles punctuated by boss encounters. Early game heavily emphasizes dungeon maze navigation and resource gathering to build power before tower ascents. Mid-to-late game shifts to structured tower climbing with boss gates. Nonlinear structure allows player-controlled pacing but requires persistence through lengthy hidden dungeons.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Invisible portals to Parallel Zones and Secret Rooms hidden throughout towers; players must explore exhaustively or stumble upon them. Secret areas contain superior equipment incentivizing thorough exploration.
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Invisible portals serve as gateways to secret content; finding hidden dungeons requires entering unmarked doorways in initial exploration area

difficulty_curve.early_game: Moderate-to-high. Prince Myer begins very weak (single sword projectile, enemies take 8+ hits). Players must grind ludder currency and navigate 10 hidden dungeons (first dungeon has 167 screens) with invisible entrances to reach main palace. High friction from weak starting state.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: High. Bell towers introduce vertical scrolling sections and boss encounters. Each of seven towers must be climbed and boss defeated to collect bells. Equipment upgrades from shops and secret rooms available but require currency management and exploration.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Very high. Final boss (Rubas) accessible only after burning all seven bells. Requires players to have accumulated sufficient equipment and power-ups through the entire sequence. Nonlinear structure means players can attempt towers in varied orders, but final confrontation is gated.
notable_spikes: Initial weak state before first equipment purchases
Hidden dungeon 10 (235 screens) before accessing main palace
Each boss encounter at tower tops

notable_level_patterns.description: Ten increasingly large hidden dungeons (167-235 screens) must be traversed before accessing main palace. Dungeons have invisible entrances and exits in specific rooms. Serves as extended resource-gathering and power-up phase.

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Vertical Tower Climb with Boss Gate
notable_level_patterns.description: Each of seven bell towers scrolls vertically. Boss awaits at top. Upon defeat, bell is collected. Bell must be returned to sacred flame to destroy tower and progress.
Main palace contains one long horizontal scrolling room with sacred flame in center and entrances to all seven bell towers branching from it. Serves as hub structure.
Parallel Zones and Secret Rooms hidden via invisible portals throughout towers. Optional high-risk exploration rewarded with superior equipment. Encourages thorough play and repeat visits.
